claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm here to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children I'd like to ask about - Alex who's 3, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 2. They all live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have three children - Alex (3 years old), Blake (8 years old), and Charlie (2 years old) - who all live at home with you.

Now I need to ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your immigration status and right to reside:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another immigration status in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now let me ask about your responsibility for the children:

**Are you the person primarily responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie? By this I mean, do you provide for their day-to-day needs such as food, clothes, and general care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I look after all three of them every day - I provide their meals, buy their clothes, take them to school, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions to make sure I have all the information:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Are any of your children currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period, or are any of them being financially supported by your local council?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in hospital or care or anything like that. They're all at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Here's the summary for each child:

✅ **Alex (age 3)** - Eligible  
✅ **Blake (age 8)** - Eligible  
✅ **Charlie (age 2)** - Eligible

You meet all the key requirements:
- You're a British citizen living in the UK
- All three children are under 16
- They all live with you
- You're responsible for their day-to-day care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them
- None of them are in hospital or care

**Important things to note:**

1. You can claim Child Benefit for all three children, but only one person can claim for each child.

2. If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can use the Child Benefit tax calculator to work this out.

3. You can make a claim for Child Benefit through the official government process.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside, the claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care including food and clothes, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in hospital or care. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside, the claimant is responsible for Blake's day-to-day care including food and clothes, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and Blake is not in hospital or care. Blake meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside, the claimant is responsible for Charlie's day-to-day care including food and clothes, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, and Charlie is not in hospital or care. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with three children under 16 (Alex age 3, Blake age 8, and Charlie age 2) who all live at home. The claimant is responsible for all three children's day-to-day care and needs, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them, and none are in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for all three children.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside, the claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care including food and clothes, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in hospital or care. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside, the claimant is responsible for Blake's day-to-day care including food and clothes, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and Blake is not in hospital or care. Blake meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside, the claimant is responsible for Charlie's day-to-day care including food and clothes, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, and Charlie is not in hospital or care. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with three children under 16 (Alex age 3, Blake age 8, and Charlie age 2) who all live at home. The claimant is responsible for all three children's day-to-day care and needs, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them, and none are in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for all three children.
